posture: Quiet Sunday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the week's strongest 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the §1a-§1d free sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Several recurring or undated items were again considered and excluded: the NSW statewide FOGO mandate (large-business phase commenced 1 July 2026) and related kerbside-standardisation milestones are regulatory-rollout dates rather than fresh innovation signals; ongoing NSW AI-in-planning activity (the multi-council Early Adopter AI trial, DAISY development-application assistant) produced no new dated development today; the NSW EPA Local Government Waste Solutions Fund round-four grants (~$2.3M across ten councils for problematic waste such as batteries, white goods and mattresses) remain undated in-window and traceable to earlier reporting; recycled-content road/pavement material (Reconophalt soft-plastic-and-glass asphalt, City of Sydney geopolymer pavement, Sutherland Shire recycled-roads trial) surfaced only via older or undated coverage; and overseas material (Bloomberg CityLab 2026 in Madrid, UK LGA Innovation Zone / LGIU finance and reorganisation coverage, LGA-MHCLG digital-skills plan) was either out-of-window or general policy rather than a fresh, council-transferable signal. Today is a Sunday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
